approach: CR names the fix shape and the requester settled the semantics live across two corrections, so the design needs no invention

purely additive: the only frozen scenario touching this field pins 'the manifest's workspace is null on tmux', which stays true because tmux has no workspace tier — nothing narrowed, no Clearance floor

the seam widens by an OPTIONAL return field, so an existing implementor returning only a pane id still satisfies the contract — not a breaking change, and the package is pre-1.0 regardless, so the Compatibility ceiling is not approached
no plugin registry present — SDD default production chain for every touched artifact-type
every backend claim is probed against live herdr 0.7.4 and tmux 3.6b rather than inferred from docs, matching this project's standing bar

what: No mechanical guard checks that a .feature parses. The repo's verify task is build + typecheck + lint + test + formatter; none of them reads a .feature, so a frozen suite sat unparseable across multiple CRs while every gate reported green. A frozen suite that no tool can read cannot be mechanically enforced, and the freeze guard's own structural diff silently degrades to an all-zero summary against an unparseable base rather than failing loudly. Proposal: add a parse check over every .feature to the verify task, and make the freeze guard treat an unparseable base as an error rather than as no-change.
discovered-while: running the freeze guard's structural diff for this CR, which returned all-zero because the base would not parse

what: A bare `open --format json` reports the pane alone, so the workspace the seam now returns reaches a consumer only through the layout manifest. A caller opening a single pane without a layout — the common case — still cannot tell which workspace it landed in, though the backend said so and the seam now carries it. Proposal: widen the plain open report to carry the workspace, on the same absent-on-a-backend-with-no-workspace-tier convention. Deliberately not settled in this CR, whose scope was the manifest fault the source issue named.
discovered-while: cold impl-judge's structural read at the impl gate

why.floor: none — purely additive. Structural scenario-set diff against the merge-base: 4 added, 0 removed, 0 modified, so no Clearance floor. The one frozen scenario touching this field pins the manifest's workspace as null on tmux, which stays true because tmux has no workspace tier. Compatibility: the seam widens by an OPTIONAL return field, so an implementor returning only a pane id still satisfies it — non-breaking, and pre-1.0 regardless.
why.blast: medium — two behavioral nodes (mux, the seam; layout, where the fault surfaced). The worktree report is deliberately untouched, which is what keeps every frozen worktree scenario true.
why.novelty: medium — the CR named the fix shape, but the requester overturned two of my own proposals during the grill: a two-field binding/occupancy model (over-modeled — one workspace tier, two relations, reported by separate outputs) and a claimed degraded worktree --layout case (a phantom — worktree add --layout hardcodes the workspace placement, independently confirmed by the judge at cli.ts:580). Every backend claim probed against live herdr 0.7.4, never inferred.
why.confidence: high — the freeze guard's structural diff was caught returning an all-zero summary against an unparseable base rather than a clean additive one; isolated the real edit class by repairing a base copy and comparing scenario sets. Self-asserted (by agent) — ratify or kick back.

why.floor: none — no frozen scenario narrowed; built to the suite, and the suite was not edited to fit the code. The one implementation-shaped scenario change was a WORDING repair the judge forced, not a contract move.
why.blast: medium — a widened seam plus a one-line manifest fix, on a branch rebased onto the target so the gate judged the tree that lands.
why.novelty: medium — the fault is real and was reproduced live: panes demonstrably sitting in a workspace while the manifest reported null.
why.confidence: high — cold sdd-impl-judge verified all 8 frozen scenarios against LIVE herdr 0.7.4 and tmux 3.6b by driving the built bin, not by trusting the mocked Exec; it built its own mutation backstop proving the probe catches the pre-CR fault and restored the tree byte-identical by hash, and PATH-shimmed herdr to prove the no-extra-call claim by counting real invocations. It independently confirmed the scope claim that worktree add --layout needed no change. pnpm verify --force green, 424 tests. Its one substantive catch — added scenarios phrased as CLI reports when a bare open --format json emits the pane alone, making them true only under a seam reading — was corrected rather than shipped, and the gap it exposed filed as a follow-up. Self-asserted (by agent) — ratify or kick back.

supersedes: the backlog followup in this shard about the plain open report omitting the workspace
what: RESOLVED IN THIS CR — do not file. The requester folded it in on discovering the fact needed no lookup: it was already in hand at the report call site and being discarded. open --format json now carries { pane, workspace }, null on a backend with no workspace tier, with the text report omitting the line rather than printing a bare null. A later drain must skip the superseded line rather than file an issue for work already landed.
